title: Tragacantha fragrans
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Tragacantha fragrans. However, I found information on Tragacanth (also known as Tragacantha or Astragalus gummifer), a perennial shrub native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions.

Here's a paragraph about Tragacanth:

Tragacanth is a plant that produces a gummy sap, which is used as a thickening agent in various applications. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include gelling agents, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tragacanth is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as cough syrups, ointments, and salves, as well as a thickening agent in pharmaceuticals and food products.
taxonomy: Tragacantha fragrans is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Tragacantha fragrans is further divided into several species, each with unique features and adaptations. The genus Tragacantha is considered to be part of the subfamily Faboideae, which comprises a large group of flowering plants. The classification of Tragacantha fragrans is constantly evolving as new research and discoveries are made.
uses: Tragacantha fragrans is a plant species with potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. The plant contains saponins, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues.
